codeReview.summary: The fix closes the prior blocking gap by replacing the old non-empty-string credential gate with `validateSheetsCredentialReadiness()`, then wiring that readiness result into both `/health` and discovery preflight before any write attempt begins. The new helper validates the active credential source in the same precedence order as the sheet writer (access token, service-account JSON/file, OAuth token JSON/file), and the reviewed tests plus handoff evidence show the existing async status and grounded-source warning behavior still remain intact. I found no blocking or non-blocking code defects in scope for this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The base worker cleanup guidance still appears to conflict with this mission's documented dirty-baseline rule, so this fix worker had to record another justified procedure deviation just to commit the feature without disturbing unrelated local changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/emilionunezgarcia/.factory/missions/62d73272-e50b-4b4e-89d1-be82508ac1ac/AGENTS.md:7-10 documents the intentionally dirty baseline and forbids cleaning unrelated changes; /Users/emilionunezgarcia/.factory/missions/62d73272-e50b-4b4e-89d1-be82508ac1ac/handoffs/2026-04-10T09-37-37-787Z__discovery-worker-credential-preflight-hardening__b5976515-0b15-4220-a5b8-969937f2eae5.json:87-92 records `followedProcedure: false` for step `3.2 Environment Cleanup` for exactly that reason.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature entry, prior failing review, handoff, transcript skeleton, shared-state docs, and commit `7200099e10715073a12eca85eebfda0276a41cdd`. The new credential-readiness helper is now used by both `integrations/browser-use-discovery/src/server.ts` health reporting and `integrations/browser-use-discovery/src/webhook/handle-discovery-webhook.ts` preflight, so missing credential files and malformed credential JSON fail early and explicitly instead of being reported as ready until the sheet-write path crashes. The prior blocking issue from `discovery-worker-readiness-and-write-contract` is adequately addressed.
